methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Diraq is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Andrew Dzurak that has raised $100M+ to date. The quantum sector comp universe includes three public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS); private comparables are absent, limiting peer benchmarking options.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement. Using the $100M total raised as a value proxy and applying this multiplier yields an implied valuation of $200M. This single-method approach reflects the scarcity of directly comparable private quantum firms and the difficulty of extracting relevant revenue or growth multiples from public quantum peers operating at different stages and with different business models.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $170M to $230M, with a median of $200M. Key uncertainties include the applicability of public quantum multiples to a pre-revenue or early-stage private company, the representativeness of the 2.0x Series A step-up in the quantum sector specifically, and the absence of private peer data to corroborate implied values. The narrow methodology base underscores that this valuation is provisional pending additional comparable transaction data or clearer revenue visibility.
